message:
fix: add CJK measure_text tests, LayoutConstraint import, remove dup test

- Re-add 3 CJK display-width tests for measure_text in mermaid_layout.rs
- Add LayoutConstraint import for hook-introduced constraint functions
- Add constraints field to MermaidDiagramIr test constructors
- Remove duplicate buggy write_log_ui_fills_terminal_is_noop test body
  in terminal_writer.rs (hooks inserted fixed version but left old code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3nkw2","title":"Markdown Integration: Mermaid Fence Rendering","description":"Background
- Markdown renderer currently shows raw Mermaid code fences.

Scope
- Detect ```mermaid fences in MarkdownRenderer.
- Parse Mermaid content and render via TME pipeline.
- Apply init directives and config overrides where allowed.
- Apply link policy and render hyperlinks/footnotes when enabled.
- On parse/layout errors, render structured error panel (with location) and optional raw snippet.
- Feature flag to enable/disable Mermaid rendering.
- Log render outcomes to JSONL (success/error, size, tier, warnings).

Considerations
- Rendering must respect available width/height for the panel.
- Errors must not crash or poison caches.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests for Mermaid rendering in Markdown screen.
- Error-panel snapshot tests for malformed Mermaid.

Success Criteria
- Mermaid fences display diagrams, not raw code.
- Errors render as readable panels with location -{"id":"bd-3nohm","title":"Fix lexer byte-to-char casts for non-ASCII UTF-8 support","description":"The Mermaid lexer uses 'b as char' casts at 6 locations which incorrectly handle non-ASCII bytes. Multi-byte UTF-8 characters (CJK node names, emoji) get split into separate invalid Punct tokens. Fix: decode full UTF-8 chars and handle non-ASCII in identifier lexing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:42.986648885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:12.270008890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:12.269990645Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":788,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will fix the 6 byte-to-char cast locations in the Mermaid lexer to properly handle non-ASCII UTF-8 characters.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:18:00Z"},{"id":789,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed lexer byte-to-char casts: added decode_current_char()/advance_char() for UTF-8, updated is_ident_start/is_ident_continue for Unicode letters, fixed next_token/lex_identifier/lex_arrow_or_punct to branch on ASCII vs non-ASCII. Added tokenize_non_ascii_identifiers test. All 402 mermaid tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:08Z"}]}

Will fix the 6 byte-to-char cast locations in the Mermaid lexer to properly handle non-ASCII UTF-8 characters.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:18:00Z"},{"id":789,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed lexer byte-to-char casts: added decode_current_char()/advance_char() for UTF-8, updated is_ident_start/is_ident_continue for Unicode letters, fixed next_token/lex_identifier/lex_arrow_or_punct to branch on ASCII vs non-ASCII. Added tokenize_non_ascii_identifiers test. All 402 mermaid tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:08Z"},{"id":790,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Fixed. The core bug (non-ASCII bytes split into separate Punct tokens) was already fixed by a previous agent. I additionally:
1. Fixed column tracking in lex_string() and lex_comment_or_directive() — non-ASCII chars inside strings/comments now advance by 1 column instead of N bytes, via new advance_byte_or_char() helper
2. Added 6 comprehensive non-ASCII tests: CJK identifiers, CJK strings, emoji strings, emoji as Punct, CJK comments, and span column counting verification
3. Fixed pre-existing compilation errors: added missing 'constraints' field to MermaidDiagramIr constructors in mermaid.rs, dot_parser.rs, and diagram_layout_bench.rs
All 486 ftui-extras tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:26:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection","description":"## Background
Mirror/reflection creates a classic 'glossy' text effect with a vertically flipped copy below. Works especially well for titles using AsciiArtText, terminal-appropriate since it uses vertical space.

## Implementation

Add Reflection struct (separate from TextEffect for flexibility):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Reflection {
    pub gap: u16,            // Rows between text and reflection (0-3 typical)
    pub opacity_start: f64,  // Top of reflection (0.3-0.5 typical)
    pub opacity_end: f64,    // Bottom of reflection (0.0 typical)
    pub height_ratio: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, how much of text height to reflect
    pub wave: f64,           // 0.0=perfect mirror, 1.0=wavy water effect
}

impl Default for Reflection {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            gap: 1,
            opacity_start: 0.4,
            opacity_end: 0.0,
            height_ratio: 0.5,  // Reflect bottom half of text
            wave: 0.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Usage with StyledText:
```rust
// Only works with multi-line text (AsciiArtText or actual multi-line)
let art = AsciiArtText::new("TITLE", AsciiArtStyle::Block);
StyledText::from_ascii_art(art)
    .reflection(Reflection::default())
    .effect(TextEffect::HorizontalGradient { ... })
```

### Single-Line Behavior:
For single-line text, reflection is a no-op (nothing to flip vertically). Use AsciiArtText for reflections on titles.

### Rendering:
```rust
fn render_with_reflection(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
    // 1. Render main text
    self.render_text(main_area, frame);
    
    // 2. Calculate reflection area
    let reflect_height = (self.text_height as f64 * self.reflection.height_ratio) as u16;
    let reflect_y = main_area.bottom() + self.reflection.gap;
    
    // 3. Render flipped text with gradient opacity
    for row in 0..reflect_height {
        let source_row = self.text_height - 1 - row;  // Flip vertically
        let opacity = lerp(self.reflection.opacity_start, 
                          self.reflection.opacity_end,
                          row as f64 / reflect_height as f64);
        
        let wave_offset = if self.reflection.wave > 0.0 {
            (row as f64 * 0.5 + self.time * 2.0).sin() * self.reflection.wave
        } else { 0.0 };
        
        self.render_row_with_opacity(source_row, reflect_y + row, opacity, wave_offset, frame);
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reflection_single_line_noop: No reflection for 1-row text
- test_reflection_multiline_flips: Rows rendered in reverse order
- test_reflection_gap_applied: Correct spacing between text and reflection
- test_reflection_opacity_gradient: Top brighter, bottom fainter
- test_reflection_height_ratio: Only reflects specified portion
- test_reflection_wave_offsets: Horizontal shimmer with wave > 0
- test_reflection_inherits_effects: Gradient text = gradient reflection

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-row AsciiArt 'HELLO' with reflection
- Snapshot with wave=0.5 showing water ripple effect
- Snapshot gradient text with reflection (both should be gradient)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

Added reflected_rows(), wave shimmer, gap spacing. 5 new tests + 3 updated.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:07:09.767617641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:24.700806595Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:24.700773683Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["text-effects"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o1l","depends_on_id":"bd-2bzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig","title":"Mermaid Mega Showcase Screen (interactive layout lab)","description":"Purpose:
- Detailed breakdown for bd-2nkmi (Mermaid Showcase Super Screen). This epic decomposes the work into granular, testable tasks.

Goal:
- Add a brand-new, over-the-top, super-interactive Mermaid demo screen that showcases every Mermaid feature we already support, plus live layout recomputation under size/complexity changes.

Why:
- The demo showcase is the primary product lens; this screen should prove correctness, determinism, and performance of our Mermaid pipeline under stress.
- We need a single place to show complex diagrams, layout recalculation speed, and debug instrumentation.

Scope:
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI, multiple diagram families, live controls, and performance panels.
- Curated + parameterized samples, layout/route/debug overlays, and a polished help/legend panel.
- Unit tests, snapshot tests, and E2E scripts with explicit logging.

Non-goals:
- Rewriting the Mermaid parser or introducing new diagram syntax not already supported.

Acceptance:
- A dedicated screen is selectable in the demo showcase and is stable across multiple sizes.
- Users can change sample, size, layout knobs, and see recomputation timings and layout stability.
- Tests provide deterministic coverage of samples, layout behavior, and input handling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:30:50.484372892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:10.315217603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:03.069977897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.1","title":"Mega screen feature matrix + scope map","description":"Goal:
- Inventory Mermaid features already implemented and map them to concrete demo coverage.

Deliverables:
- A written feature matrix in-code (doc comment or static data) that lists: diagram families, supported syntax features, and which sample demonstrates each feature.
- Explicit gaps/unknowns captured as TODOs in the matrix.
- Coverage counts per family (features covered vs total supported).

Considerations:
- Focus on existing capabilities only; do not invent unsupported syntax.
- Include stress/perf features (large graphs, dense edge labels, multi-line nodes).
- Matrix must be used by sample registry; this prevents samples from drifting away from stated feature coverage.

Acceptance:
- Matrix is easy to update and referenced by the mega screen sample registry.
- Every planned sample has a declared purpose and linked feature coverage.
- A small unit test validates that each sample references only features present in the matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:47:42.798825967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:44:16.523237899Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":748,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented feature matrix as FEATURE_MATRIX const in mermaid.rs with 40 entries covering all 8 diagram types plus cross-cutting features. Each entry maps: family, feature name, support level (Supported/Partial/Unsupported), test fixture path, and notes. Added helper functions: features_for_type(), feature_coverage_summary(), uncovered_features(). Added 8 tests for matrix completeness, type coverage, palette roundtrip, and config hash. 450 tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:44:12Z"},{"id":751,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentBrook: Completed. Added comprehensive feature matrix as in-code documentation (100+ line comment table mapping diagram families, syntax features, layout directions, rendering features, and stress/perf scenarios to samples). Added KNOWN_FEATURE_TAGS (35 tags) and FEATURE_GAPS (13 documented gaps) static arrays. Added 4 matrix validation tests ensuring tag consistency. 100 mermaid tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:48:35Z"}]}

- Cache state is visible in the metrics panel and included in structured logs.
- Unit test verifies invalidation for each control group.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:39.985281747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:19.362139953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:18:26.910324906Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","render","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:39.985281747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:36.659120972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:39.102918339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":758,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented render pipeline integration and cache invalidation for the Mermaid Mega Showcase screen:

- Added MegaRenderCache struct with IR, layout, buffer, epoch tracking, cache hit/miss counters, and timing metrics (parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms)
- Added RefCell<MegaRenderCache> to MermaidMegaShowcaseScreen for interior mutability (view takes &self)
- Implemented ensure_render_cache() with 3-tier invalidation: analysis (parse+IR), layout, render — matching the existing mermaid_showcase.rs pattern
- Implemented blit_buffer() for centering the cached buffer onto the frame
- Added 10 curated inline Mermaid samples (Flow Basic/Subgraphs/Dense, Sequence, Class, State, ER, Pie, Gantt, Mindmap)
- Added to_config() layout mode support (Dense=400 iter budget, Spacious=140)
- Added layout_spacing() with Dense (tight 2.0 gaps) and Spacious (loose 6.0 gaps)
- Updated side panel to display cache metrics: parse/layout/render timing, cache hits/misses, last cache status
- Replaced render_diagram_placeholder with render_diagram using the full pipeline
- Added 7 new tests (mega_samples_non_empty, selected_source_wraps_around, render_cache_starts_empty, ensure_cache_populates_on_first_call, ensure_cache_hits_on_repeat, config_dense_has_higher_budget, layout_spacing_dense_tighter)
- All 23 mega showcase tests + all 1155 demo showcase tests pass, clippy clean","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:18:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.8","title":"Live layout recomputation controls + throttling","description":"Goal:
- Allow users to change size/complexity knobs and watch layout recompute without UI jitter.

Deliverables:
- Controls for size, spacing, layout mode, and orientation.
- Throttling/coalescing rules for expensive recomputes (debounce + latest-wins).
- UI indicator when recompute is pending or in-flight.

Acceptance:
- Rapid keypresses coalesce into a single layout pass.
- UI remains responsive while recompute is in flight.
- E2E logs show throttle events with timestamps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:43.687494913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:23.283809323Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","perf","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:43.687494913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.5.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:48.014885368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:42.600700153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:45.027063511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.9","title":"Optimal layout mode + auto-fit/auto-scale","description":"Goal:
- Provide an "optimal layout" mode that chooses spacing and scale to best fit the viewport.

Deliverables:
- Auto-fit heuristics (minimize overflow, reduce edge crossings if available).
- Layout quality scoring (overflow, crossings, edge length variance) used to pick the best candidate.
- Toggle for auto-fit vs manual size.

Acceptance:
- Switching to optimal mode recomputes layout and visibly improves fit in most cases.
- Quality scores are surfaced in the metrics panel for transparency.
- Auto-fit behavior is deterministic and documented in the help panel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:48.557849994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:38.337127619Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:32:48.557849994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:55.661961616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:52.827013258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3obms","title":"Improved Crossing Minimization (Sifting Algorithm)","description":"## Goal
Augment or replace the barycenter heuristic in diagram_layout.rs with the sifting algorithm for better edge crossing reduction. The existing barycenter approach is correct but produces suboptimal layouts for dense graphs (10+ nodes per layer).

## Background
- Current: barycenter_sort does bidirectional sweeps using neighbor position averages. O(n*E) per sweep, good results for sparse graphs.
- Sifting: for each node in a layer, try inserting it at every position and pick the one with minimum crossings. O(n^2 * E) per layer but finds significantly better solutions.
- The layout quality metrics already measure crossings — improvement is directly measurable.

## Implementation
1. Add sifting_sort() alongside barycenter_sort() in diagram_layout.rs.
2. For each layer, for each node: remove from position, count crossings at each alternate position, insert at best position.
3. Use the optimized O(1) crossing counter (the ref_member boolean array) from the barycenter fix.
4. Run sifting after barycenter (as a refinement pass) rather than as a replacement.
5. Budget-gated: sifting is more expensive; respect the computation budget.

## Expected Improvement
- 15-30% fewer crossings on graphs with 20+ nodes (based on graph drawing literature).
- No change for simple graphs (barycenter already optimal for small inputs).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (add sifting_sort, integrate with minimize_crossings)

## Tests
- Unit: sifting produces <= crossings compared to barycenter alone
- Property: sifting output is a valid permutation of the layer

+{"id":"bd-3obms","title":"Improved Crossing Minimization (Sifting Algorithm)","description":"## Goal
Augment or replace the barycenter heuristic in diagram_layout.rs with the sifting algorithm for better edge crossing reduction. The existing barycenter approach is correct but produces suboptimal layouts for dense graphs (10+ nodes per layer).

## Background
- Current: barycenter_sort does bidirectional sweeps using neighbor position averages. O(n*E) per sweep, good results for sparse graphs.
- Sifting: for each node in a layer, try inserting it at every position and pick the one with minimum crossings. O(n^2 * E) per layer but finds significantly better solutions.
- The layout quality metrics already measure crossings — improvement is directly measurable.

## Implementation
1. Add sifting_sort() alongside barycenter_sort() in diagram_layout.rs.
2. For each layer, for each node: remove from position, count crossings at each alternate position, insert at best position.
3. Use the optimized O(1) crossing counter (the ref_member boolean array) from the barycenter fix.
4. Run sifting after barycenter (as a refinement pass) rather than as a replacement.
5. Budget-gated: sifting is more expensive; respect the computation budget.

## Expected Improvement
- 15-30% fewer crossings on graphs with 20+ nodes (based on graph drawing literature).
- No change for simple graphs (barycenter already optimal for small inputs).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (add sifting_sort, integrate with minimize_crossings)

## Tests
- Unit: sifting produces <= crossings compared to barycenter alone
- Property: sifting output is a valid permutation of the layer
- Benchmark: measure crossing reduction on 10 test graphs","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:48.481508990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:30:11.507392511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","optimization"],"comments":[{"id":791,"issue_id":"bd-3obms","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will implement the sifting algorithm as a refinement pass after barycenter sort in the crossing minimization pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:30:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3r87","title":"Text Effects: Scanline / CRT Effects","description":"## Background
CRT/scanline effects create strong nostalgic retro appeal, perfect for terminal UIs. Simulates the look of old monitors applied to text.

## Implementation

Add TextEffect variant:
```rust
TextEffect::Scanline {
    intensity: f64,      // 0.0-1.0, how dark the scan lines (0.3 typical)
    line_gap: u8,        // Every Nth row dimmed (2 = every other row)
    scroll: bool,        // Lines scroll slowly for CRT feel
    scroll_speed: f64,   // Rows per second if scrolling
    flicker: f64,        // 0.0-1.0, random brightness variation
}
```

### Rendering:
```rust
fn apply_scanline(&self, color: PackedRgba, row: u16, time: f64) -> PackedRgba {
    // Determine if this row is a scanline
    let scroll_offset = if self.scroll { 
        (time * self.scroll_speed) as u16 
    } else { 
        0 
    };
    let is_scanline = (row + scroll_offset) % self.line_gap as u16 == 0;
    
    // Apply dimming
    let dim_factor = if is_scanline { 1.0 - self.intensity } else { 1.0 };
    
    // Apply flicker (random per-frame variation)
    let flicker_factor = 1.0 - self.flicker * random_for_frame(time);
    
    apply_alpha(color, dim_factor * flicker_factor)
}
```

### Note on Screen Effects:
Screen-wide effects like curvature, vignette, and barrel distortion are out of scope for text effects. Those would be terminal/screen-level post-processing, not text styling.

### Phosphor Glow:
For authentic CRT look, combine Scanline with a subtle Glow effect:
```rust
StyledText::new("RETRO")
    .effect(TextEffect::Scanline { intensity: 0.3, line_gap: 2, ... })
    .effect(TextEffect::Glow { color: green, intensity: 0.2, ... })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_scanline_dims_every_nth: Correct rows affected
- test_scanline_intensity_applied: Color reduced by intensity
- test_scanline_scroll_moves_pattern: Pattern shifts with time
- test_scanline_flicker_varies: Different frames = different brightness
- test_scanline_gap_1_dims_all: Every row affected with gap=1
- test_scanline_gap_2_alternates: Every other row

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot multi-line text with scanlines, gap=2
- Snapshot same text at t=0 vs t=1 with scroll

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Scanline / CRT Effects
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
